title: fix(langchain): streaming path to apply middleware and guardrails
body_text:
This fixes the streaming bypass of middleware guardrails reported in #35011.

The problem: when using agent.stream() or agent.astream() with stream_mode="messages", raw LLM tokens are emitted to the user through StreamMessagesHandler.on_llm_new_token before any after_model middleware has a chance to inspect them. This means output-guard middleware like PIIMiddleware (with apply_to_output=True) runs too late -- PII and other sensitive content has already leaked through the stream.

The fix has two parts:

1. AgentMiddleware gets a new guards_output attribute (default False). Middleware that checks or transforms model output sets this to True. PIIMiddleware sets it when apply_to_output=True.

2. Middleware with guards_output=True implements wrap_model_call / awrap_model_call to sanitize the model response inside the model node itself, before on_chain_end emits it. create_agent detects guards_output middleware and applies TAG_NOSTREAM to the model node's RunnableCallable. This prevents StreamMessagesHandler from emitting raw per-token chunks via on_llm_new_token, while the sanitized complete messages still reach the user through on_chain_end.

The existing after_model hook is kept for backward compatibility. It's idempotent since it sees the already-sanitized content from wrap_model_call.

Files changed:
- agents/middleware/types.py: Added guards_output attribute to AgentMiddleware base class
- agents/middleware/pii.py: Added wrap_model_call, awrap_model_call, and _process_model_response to PIIMiddleware; set guards_output = apply_to_output
- agents/factory.py: Detect guards_output middleware and apply TAG_NOSTREAM to model node
- tests: Added 8 tests for the streaming guard behavior (guards_output flag, wrap_model_call sanitization, TAG_NOSTREAM tagging, invoke compatibility)

All existing tests continue to pass (59 PII tests, 566 middleware tests, 707 agent tests).

entities.description: The framework or library where the streaming bypass vulnerability was found.

entities.name: Streaming Bypass
entities.description: A vulnerability where streamed data bypasses security or processing middleware.

entities.name: Middleware Guardrails
entities.description: Security or processing layers designed to inspect and potentially modify data streams.

entities.name: Issue 35011
entities.description: The specific GitHub issue number where the streaming bypass problem was reported.

entities.name: agent.stream()
entities.description: A method used for streaming output from an agent.

entities.name: agent.astream()
entities.description: An asynchronous method used for streaming output from an agent.

entities.name: stream_mode="messages"
entities.description: A configuration setting for streaming, specifically for messages.

entities.name: Raw LLM Tokens
entities.description: Unprocessed, individual tokens emitted directly from a Large Language Model.

entities.name: StreamMessagesHandler
entities.description: A component responsible for handling and emitting streamed messages.

entities.name: on_llm_new_token
entities.description: A callback method within StreamMessagesHandler that emits raw LLM tokens.

entities.name: after_model Middleware
entities.description: Middleware designed to run after the model has processed its input.

entities.name: Output-Guard Middleware
entities.description: A type of middleware specifically designed to inspect and protect model output.

entities.name: PIIMiddleware
entities.description: A specific implementation of output-guard middleware focused on Personally Identifiable Information.

entities.name: PII
entities.description: Personally Identifiable Information, which is sensitive content.

entities.name: Sensitive Content
entities.description: Information that should not be leaked or exposed.

entities.name: AgentMiddleware
entities.description: The base class for agent middleware components.

entities.name: guards_output Attribute
entities.description: A new attribute added to AgentMiddleware to indicate if middleware checks or transforms model output.

entities.name: wrap_model_call
entities.description: A method implemented by middleware to sanitize model responses.

entities.name: awrap_model_call
entities.description: An asynchronous version of the wrap_model_call method.

entities.name: Model Response
entities.description: The output generated by the language model.

entities.name: Model Node
entities.description: A specific node within the agent's processing graph that represents the model call.

entities.name: on_chain_end
entities.description: A callback method that emits complete messages at the end of a processing chain.

entities.name: create_agent
entities.description: A function or method responsible for creating an agent instance.

entities.name: TAG_NOSTREAM
entities.description: A tag applied to prevent raw per-token streaming.

entities.name: RunnableCallable
entities.description: A callable component that can be executed within a LangChain runnable.

entities.name: _process_model_response
entities.description: An internal method used by PIIMiddleware for processing model responses.

entities.description: A file containing definitions for middleware types.

entities.name: agents/middleware/pii.py
entities.description: A file containing the implementation of PIIMiddleware.

entities.name: agents/factory.py
entities.description: A file containing the agent creation logic.

entities.name: Tests
entities.description: The test suite for the project.

entities.description: The new behavior implemented to guard streaming output.

extracted_data.summary: This fix addresses a streaming bypass vulnerability in LangChain agents where raw LLM tokens, potentially containing PII, were emitted before output-guard middleware could process them, by introducing a `guards_output` attribute to `AgentMiddleware` and applying `TAG_NOSTREAM` to prevent premature token emission.
